title: Depth Recurrence: 3x3x1024 (non-record, pending H100)
author: Marvbuster
body:
## Summary
- Non-record submission exploring **depth recurrence**: 3 unique transformer blocks repeated 3 times (effective depth 9) at dim=1024
- Trades architectural diversity for width — same parameter budget allows dim=1024 instead of baseline's 512
- 23.1M params → ~14.7MB post-training compressed (92% of 16MB budget)
- Pending H100 compute credits for official validation run

## Approach
Systematically evaluated 6 strategies (shape, attention, MLP expansion, depth recurrence, layer sharing, SwiGLU) across 40+ configurations. Depth recurrence won decisively. Then optimized across 5 architecture axes and 4 hyperparameters.

## Key changes from baseline
- Recurrent forward pass: blocks are reused across repeats (no U-Net skip connections)
- Tuned hyperparameters: matrix_lr=0.02, muon_momentum=0.85, logit_softcap=15, muon_backend_steps=7, qk_gain_init=2.0
- Lower LR compensates for gradient amplification through repeated block application

## Local results (Apple Silicon, not comparable to leaderboard)
- 1000-step benchmark: val_bpb 1.8698 vs baseline 1.9743 (+0.105 relative improvement)
- Full H100 run needed for absolute BPB comparison

## Test plan
- [ ] Obtain H100 compute credits
- [ ] Run full 10-minute training on 8xH100
- [ ] Verify post-training compressed size < 16MB
- [ ] Report final val_bpb
submission_readme:
Non-record depth recurrence submission pending H100 validation.

## Approach

3 unique transformer blocks repeated 3 times (effective depth 9) at dim=1536.
Trades architectural diversity for width. Combined with Int6 QAT and NorMuon.

## Configuration

- 3 unique blocks × 3 repeats, dim=1536, 24 heads, 12 KV-heads (GQA 2:1)
- ReLU² MLP (2× expansion), tied embeddings, logit softcap=15
- QAT Int6 STE + NorMuon + sliding window eval (stride=64)
- 51.1M params → ~15.5MB Int6+zlib compressed (97% of 16MB budget)
- Tuned: matrix_lr=0.02, muon_momentum=0.85, warmdown=3000, qk_gain=2.0

## Methodology

Systematically evaluated 6 strategies across 40+ configs. Depth recurrence won.
Added QAT+Int6 (2× more params in same budget), NorMuon (+0.009 BPB), sliding window.

## Local results (Apple Silicon, not H100)

1000-step benchmark: +0.105 BPB over baseline architecture.
Absolute BPB not comparable to leaderboard — needs full 10min H100 run.

## Status

Pending H100 compute credits. Three versioned configs prepared.
